content.problemStatement:
Let PPP, QQQ, and RRR be binary strings of equal length LLL.

**Define** F(P,Q,R)F(P,Q,R)F(P,Q,R) as the minimum total cost to transform QQQ into RRR using the
following rules:



You may flip PiP_iPi​ or QiQ_iQi​ for any index iii (1≤i≤L)(1 \le i \le L)(1≤i≤L).
Each flip changes 0↔10 \leftrightarrow 10↔1 and costs 111.




After all flips are completed, you may perform any number of **valid swaps** in the
resulting string QQQ at zero cost.




A swap of indices iii and jjj in QQQ is **valid swap** if and only if the final values
of PiP_iPi​ and PjP_jPj​ are equal.



It can be proved that it is always possible to transform QQQ into RRR using the above rules.

You are given three binary strings AAA, BBB, and CCC of length NNN.

Compute
∑1≤L≤R≤NF(A[L..R], B[L..R], C[L..R]).\sum_{1 \le L \le R \le N} F(A[L..R],\,B[L..R],\,C[L..R]).∑1≤L≤R≤N​F(A[L..R],B[L..R],C[L..R]).

Here S[L..R]S[L..R]S[L..R] denotes the substring of SSS from index LLL to RRR, i.e. the string formed by concatenating the characters at indices LLL through RRR.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN — the length of the strings.
The next three lines contain three strings A,BA, BA,B and CCC respectively.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the required value.
constraints: AAA, BBB, CCC are binary strings
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 10510^5105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
For the fourth test case, we need to sum F(A[L..R],B[L..R],C[L..R])F(A[L..R],B[L..R],C[L..R])F(A[L..R],B[L..R],C[L..R]) over all substrings.


Consider the substring [3,3][3,3][3,3]:

Here B3=B_3 =B3​= 1 and C3=C_3 =C3​= 0, so flip B3B_3B3​ (cost 111).
Now B[3..3]B[3..3]B[3..3] becomes equal to C[3..3]C[3..3]C[3..3].
Hence, F(A[3..3],B[3..3],C[3..3])=1F(A[3..3],B[3..3],C[3..3]) = 1F(A[3..3],B[3..3],C[3..3])=1.



Consider the substring [2,4][2,4][2,4]:

B[2..4]=B[2..4] =B[2..4]= 111 and C[2..4]=C[2..4] =C[2..4]= 101.
Flip B3B_3B3​ from 1 to 0 (cost 111).
Now B[2..4]B[2..4]B[2..4] becomes equal to C[2..4]C[2..4]C[2..4].
Hence, F(A[2..4],B[2..4],C[2..4])=1F(A[2..4],B[2..4],C[2..4]) = 1F(A[2..4],B[2..4],C[2..4])=1.



Consider the substring [1,5][1,5][1,5]:

Flip B1B_1B1​ from 1 to 0 (cost 111).
Flip B3B_3B3​ from 1 to 0 (cost 111).
Flip B4B_4B4​ from 1 to 0 (cost 111).
Now B=B =B= 01001. Swap indices 444 and 555 in BBB.
This swap is valid since A4=A5=A_4 = A_5 =A4​=A5​= 0, so it costs 000.
After the swap, BBB becomes 01010, which is equal to CCC.
Hence, F(A[1..5],B[1..5],C[1..5])=3F(A[1..5],B[1..5],C[1..5]) = 3F(A[1..5],B[1..5],C[1..5])=3.



Calculating for all such substring, we achieve cost 191919.
